title: Whitaker v. Cole — partnership dissolution and accounting
legal_issues: partner's duty of loyalty and good faith
valuation of partnership assets on winding up
right to formal accounting under partnership statute
relief_sought: Decree dissolving Whitaker & Cole Real Estate Partners effective date of judgment
Formal accounting of all partnership receipts and disbursements from January 1, 2023
Disgorgement of approximately $310,000 in undisclosed management fees taken by defendant
Equitable distribution of remaining partnership assets after winding up
key_facts: Parties formed a 50/50 general partnership in 2018 to acquire and lease commercial properties.
Defendant managed day-to-day operations and bank accounts; plaintiff supplied 65% of initial capital.
Plaintiff discovered in October 2025 that defendant paid himself unbudgeted 'management fees' totaling $310,000 over three years without partner consent.
Partnership agreement requires unanimous consent for partner compensation above an agreed draw schedule.

contract_clauses.name: Partner compensation
contract_clauses.text: Neither Partner shall receive salary, fee, or other compensation from the Partnership except as expressly approved in writing by both Partners.

contract_clauses.name: Books and records
contract_clauses.text: Each Partner shall have full access at all times to the Partnership's books, records, and bank accounts.
